channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Alberta Cabinet Changes After Finance and Hospitals Ministers Resign
item.description: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith has announced a cabinet shuffle following the resignation of two key ministers. Former Finance Minister Nate Horner and Hospitals Minister Matt Jones stepped down on Wed...

item.title: Third Phase of Sahel Social Protection Program Aims to Strengthen Communities and Jobs
item.description: The Sahel Adaptive Social Protection Program has entered its third phase, bringing more support and job opportunities to communities in one of Africa’s most vulnerable regions. This expansion targets ...

item.title: Tanzania to host 12th African Engineering Conference in Dar es Salaam
item.description: Tanzania is getting ready to host the 12th African Engineering Conference in Dar es Salaam from September 13 to 17, 2026. Dis big event is expected to bring together over 1,000 engineers, academics, g...

item.title: Authorities begin verification of foreign nationals at Diakonia Refugee Centre amid rising tensions
item.description: The National Joint Operational and Intelligence Structure (NATJoints) has started a verification process for all foreign nationals currently staying at the Diakonia Refugee Centre in eThekwini. This a...

item.title: The 1910 Paige-Detroit Model B Roadster and Its Innovative Two-Cycle Engine
item.description: The 1910 Paige-Detroit Model B Roadster represents an important chapter in the early history of American automobile manufacturing. Introduced by the newly formed Paige-Detroit Motor Car Company, this ...

item.title: Dennis Hopper's Photographic Journey Through 1960s America
item.description: Before Dennis Hopper became known as a rebellious icon of New Hollywood and the director of 'Easy Rider,' he faced a period of professional blacklisting after a fallout with director Henry Hathaway. D...

item.title: Minor reshuffle in House committee leadership approved
item.description: The House of Representatives recently approved a series of adjustments in the leadership of some of its committees. On Wednesday, the plenary endorsed the motion filed by Batangas Representative Ryan ...

item.title: Security Forces Eliminate 23 Militants in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Operations
item.description: Security forces in Pakistan have successfully conducted a series of intelligence-based operations (IBOs) over the past 48 hours in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, targeting areas such as Datta Khel, Spinwam, and ...

item.title: PM Shehbaz acknowledges strong and enduring support of China in Pakistan-China relations
item.description: During a ceremony in Islamabad marking the 75th anniversary of diplomatic relations between Pakistan and China, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif emphasised the unwavering support China has provided to Pa...

item.title: Violent Incidents Target Canadian Waste Company Amid Construction Contract Disputes
item.description: Over the past year and a half, GFL Environmental, a major Canadian waste management company, and its sister company GIP have been the focus of a series of violent attacks. These incidents, occurring a...

item.title: Huda Mustafa and Louis Russell part ways to focus on family and careers
item.description: Love Island star Huda Mustafa and her partner Louis Russell have officially broken up, confirming rumours that had been circulating for weeks. The two, who started dating soon after Huda exited the re...

item.title: Bob Odenkirk Discusses His Career, Satire, and Life After a Heart Attack
item.description: Bob Odenkirk, widely recognised for his role as the cunning lawyer Saul Goodman in 'Better Call Saul', opens up about his experiences in the entertainment industry and his personal reflections. In a c...

item.title: Sjogren’s Disease: Recognising Early Signs and Recommended Tests for Women
item.description: Louise Mountford, 66, experienced severe and unexplained gut symptoms for over four years, including diarrhoea, constipation, bloating, and difficulty swallowing dry food. Despite multiple hospital vi...

item.title: Stellenbosch FC Maintains Presence Despite MTN8 Hopes Fading
item.description: Stellenbosch FC’s quest for a top-eight finish in the Betway Premiership is now precarious ahead of the final weekend of the season. While Gavin Hunt’s side secured a win over Orbit College FC at the ...

item.title: Understanding Misfluencers and Their Role in Online Misinformation
item.description: For online world nowadays, wahala no dey just dey for algorithm or technical side. One big issue wey dey increase na the rise of 'misfluencers' — people wey fit shape how others dey interpret, trust, ...

item.title: Dunkin’ introduces 48-ounce beverage buckets across participating US stores
item.description: Dunkin’ Donuts is set to release its 48-ounce cold beverage buckets nationwide this Friday, following a successful trial in Massachusetts and New Hampshire earlier this year. The buckets, which featur...

item.title: ICC rejects Duterte’s request to appeal confirmation of charges, trial to proceed
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) Pre-Trial Chamber I, in its decision on May 21, 2026, denied former Philippine president Rodrigo Duterte’s request to appeal the confirmation of his charges, eff...

item.title: Prenatal Exposure to Chlorpyrifos May Cause Lasting Brain Changes and Motor Impairments in Children
item.description: A recent study conducted by researchers from Columbia University's Mailman School of Public Health, Children's Hospital Los Angeles, and the Keck School of Medicine of USC reveals that prenatal exposu...

item.title: Plant-Based Diet Linked to Reduced Weight Gain in Midlife Women During Menopause
item.description: A new study led by researchers at Harvard Medical School suggests that midlife women can reduce weight gain during menopause by following a plant-based diet. The research monitored 38,283 American nur...

item.title: Early Detection of Thyroid Cancer in Women: Symptoms, Risks, and Treatments
item.description: Thyroid cancer often develops quietly, with subtle or no early symptoms, making it easy to overlook. Common early signs can include a small lump in the neck, voice changes, or difficulty swallowing, t...
